title: Prioritize Search Over Manual Scrolling for Pattern Matching
description: Avoid manual scrolling when searching for files with specific prefixes or names in large directories; use the Search function instead.
content: When the goal involves locating files matching a specific pattern (e.g., a filename prefix), manually scrolling through a file list is inefficient and prone to losing context. Always look for and utilize the 'Search' control in the toolbar to filter files instantly, ensuring all matching items are identified quickly without getting lost in the directory tree.

title: Verify Sort Criteria Matches Task Requirements
description: Do not automatically select "Modified" sorting when the task requires "Created" sorting, as these attributes differ and the app may not offer the latter directly.
content: If the application's sort menu lacks the specific criterion requested (e.g., "Date Created"), do not blindly fall back to a similar option like "Modified Date" as it may result in incorrect ordering. Instead, inspect individual file details to determine the correct dates or use a file manager that supports the required sorting natively to ensure compliance with the task.

title: Stop Repetitive Scrolling Upon Seeing Static Content
description: Cease scrolling actions immediately if the visible file list does not change, indicating you have reached a list boundary or are stuck in a loop.
content: When navigating file lists, repeated scrolling that yields the same set of files suggests stagnation. Recognizing this pattern early prevents wasting steps; switch strategies such as using search, checking the current directory structure, or verifying if the target files exist in the expected location.

title: Prioritize Management and Privacy Sections for Specialized Features
description: Do not exhaustively tap every general settings category; instead, target account management, privacy, or administrative panels first.
content: Specialized features like invite link generation with custom parameters are rarely located in generic preference screens. Navigate directly to "Manage account," "Privacy," or server administration hubs where such controls are conventionally placed before exploring broader settings menus.

title: Verify Feature Availability Before Deep Navigation
description: Do not assume a feature exists within the current app view if it is absent from logical sections; verify platform capabilities or switch interfaces if necessary.
content: If a requested configuration option cannot be found after checking relevant management sections, the mobile application may lack native support for it. Consider verifying official documentation or switching to a desktop/web interface where advanced account features are typically fully supported.

title: Track Visited Nodes to Prevent Redundant Navigation Loops
description: Do not repeatedly tap the same screen or menu item during exploration; maintain a record of visited locations to ensure forward progress.
content: Blindly re-entering previously checked menus or tapping identical buttons without variation leads to immediate stagnation. Always log explored paths and enforce a strict rule to abandon a dead-end branch after one failed attempt, moving immediately to a new structural area of the app.

title: Locate server features within Settings > Server
description: Do not search for instance-specific features like invite codes on public profiles or external sharing dialogs; they are usually hidden within the application's settings hierarchy.
content: When looking for server or instance management features (like generating invite links), navigate to the app's Settings menu and look for a dedicated "Server" or instance name entry. Tap on this entry to access instance-specific controls instead of checking account privacy or profile pages.

title: Prevent navigation loops by staying in-app during exploration
description: Avoid repeatedly minimizing the app and tapping its launcher icon when already inside the target application, as this resets navigation state and wastes steps.
content: If a feature cannot be found immediately, continue exploring visible menus and sub-menus within the current app session before exiting to the home screen. Repeatedly opening the app from the launcher typically returns to the last viewed screen, causing redundant loops.

title: Differentiate between profile sharing and administrative invite generation
description: Do not confuse standard profile sharing options (like QR codes or copy link buttons on a user profile) with administrative invite link generators.
content: Standard profile sharing generates permanent public URLs for users. Administrative invite links with expiration dates and usage limits are configuration tasks found in system or server settings, not on user-facing profile screens.
